picks.analysis: Kansas City has the worst bullpen WHIP in baseball and the third-worst ERA. The pen allowed four homers in the last three games, posting an 8.71 ERA. The Rangers have been better in relief, but Texas allowed 5+ five times in the last eight games.

The Royals have been losing big all season. K.C.'s runline and moneyline records are the same, meaning that giving up the 1.5 runs against them isn't a big risk. While starter Seth Lugo started the year strong, May was rough, posting a 5.27 ERA and 1.683 WHIP in five starts.

Mitch Keller and Bailey Ober have been among the most reliable first-inning pitchers in baseball this season. Keller owns a 9-2 NRFI record and hasn't allowed a first-inning run in three straight starts, while Ober is 10-1 and has gone nine consecutive outings without trouble. Both offenses also struggle early.

Neither lineup has done much damage in the first inning lately, making this NRFI appealing. Brandon Sproat hasn't allowed a first-inning run in five straight starts, while Peter Lambert owns a 6-1 NRFI record and a sub-4.00 ERA. Milwaukee is hitting just .205 in opening frames this season.
The Miami Marlins are tied for 27th in home runs and have posted an ISO of .117 against right-handed pitching this month, ranking them 29th in the majors.

The New York Mets have the pitching advantage with Christian Scott taking the bump. He owns a 2.8 FIP and 3.0 ERA over the past month despite a .328 batting average on balls put in play, which should drop moving forward.

The Cardinals' six-run outburst on Friday looks like an anomaly. Nelson Velasquez, called up earlier in the day, hit St. Louis' first home run this week. He's a platoon slugger, however, who'll likely be on the bench against a right-hander. That leaves the Cardinals' batters who have produced 0 or 1 runs in five of the last 10 games.

A converted reliever, Brown started his last four, never allowing more than three runs. All Brown's stuff ranks in the top third of MLB, including a 99th-percentile breaking ball. He'll face St. Louis, which scored two runs in three games before Friday. The Cardinals start Kyle Leahy, who ranks in the bottom third in every pitch and is worse than 92% of MLB pitchers in overall run value.

Jesus Luzardo has superb underlying metrics and strong prior results against LA's lineup (.168 AVG, .558 OPS). Both pens have been dominant (Philadelphia 2.07 SIERA, LA 2.96 FIP in the last 20 days) and are very well rested.

Roki Sasaki allows loud contact (11th percentile barrel rate, 11th percentile hard-hit rate), which is a concern against a Philadelphia lineup with the fifth-highest hard-hit rate (35.6%) in the last 20 days. His four-seamer has been crushed (.348 xBA, .614 xSLG), and Philadelphia (fourth-most WAR against fastballs) will be happy to oblige.

Drew Rasmussen vs. the New York Yankees is a mismatch on paper - the righty is 4-1 with a 1.03 ERA and 51 punchouts in eight career games against New York. Coming off a thrilling comeback win on Friday, the division-leading Tampa Bay Rays are primed to keep the foot on the gas behind their dynamic arm to create more separation in the AL East.

The Texas Rangers will look to keep their offense rolling after a dominant 9-1 victory over the Royals on Friday night. Kansas City’s offense has been difficult to trust lately, averaging just 2.3 runs per game while batting .203 over its last 10 games. It hasn’t just been the offense that’s struggled either, as the Royals have posted a 5.46 team ERA during that stretch. The Rangers have been much more productive lately, averaging 4.7 runs per game while hitting .240 and carrying a slightly better 4.31 ERA. Taking the mound for the Royals will be Seth Lugo, who enters with a 2-4 record, 3.74 ERA and 1.38 WHIP. For the Rangers, Kumar Rocker will get the nod, entering with a 2-5 record, 3.96 ERA and 1.36 WHIP. It’s tough to back either starter in this spot, but if Rocker runs into trouble early, Texas has a significant advantage in the bullpen. The Rangers relievers own a solid 3.11 ERA compared to Kansas City’s 5.08 bullpen ERA. While I’m not entirely confident in Rocker, I do trust this Rangers bullpen and offense a lot more than the Royals right now. Look for the Rangers to get the job done at home as the Royals struggles continue. Royals vs Rangers prediction: Texas Rangers (-125) at the time of publishing. Playable to -135. I favor the Pittsburgh Pirates over the Minnesota Twins when they face off Saturday afternoon. Pittsburgh won Game 1 of this series on Friday thanks to a 2-run walk off home run from Bryan Reynolds in what was an entertaining game with quick offensive starts from both sides and several lead changes. Mitch Keller is set to start for Pittsburgh on Saturday, marking his 12th trip to the mound this season. Through his first 64.1 innings of work, the veteran right-hander has posted a solid 3.64 ERA, and his 3.47 FIP supports that success. Furthermore, Keller has been extremely tough on right-handed batters, which should help against Byron Buxton atop Minnesota’s order. Overall, Minnesota sits 18th in wOBA with a 97 wRC+ against right-handed pitching this season. Bailey Ober is set to start for the Twins for the 12th time this year. Through 62 innings of work, the 6-foot-9 right-hander has posted a 3.92 ERA, though his 4.48 FIP is less encouraging and his recent form is not the sharpest. Ober has allowed 4 home runs in his last 2 starts and at least 1 home run in 5 of his last 6 starts. He ranks 1st percentile in fastball velocity, yet fills the zone with strikes early and often. Meanwhile, Pittsburgh has found success against low-strikeout right-handers. In fact, the Pirates sit 5th in wOBA with a 109 wRC+ against right-handed pitching. When at home, that wRC+ against righties jumps to 114. Neither bullpen is very good, but I will back the better starter and better lineup in this game. Twins vs Pirates prediction: Pittsburgh Pirates ML (-140) available at time of publishing. Playable to -145. You can bet on our Twins vs Pirates pick with BetMGM , where right now new users can activate your new BetMGM account ⚡ and receive $150 in bonus bets INSTANTLY if your first $10+ wager is a winner!

The Padres and Nationals are set for a Saturday afternoon matchup at Nationals Park in the nation’s capital. After 12 total runs were scored in the opening game of this series on Friday night in San Diego’s 7-5 win, Saturday’s contest could also be of the high-scoring variety. Washington will send right-hander Foster Griffin to the mound, and he is still adjusting to life as a starting pitcher in the big leagues. This is Griffin’s first year as a full-time starter, and he has experienced some ups-and-downs through 2 months of the season. He posted a strong 2.51 ERA in April, but that number has jumped to 4.76 across his 5 starts in May. Given his inconsistencies, look for the Padres to have a strong date at the plate in this one. Michael King will toe the rubber for the visitors. King has been a steady force on the mound this season, as he enters this matchup with a 2.76 ERA and 1.15 WHIP through 11 starts and 62.0 innings of work. However, he has a tough assignment on his hands. The Nationals have been among the best offenses in baseball so far this season, as they enter this matchup ranked 1st in total runs (311) and 4th in OPS (.745). In addition, the Nationals are scoring 5.5 runs per game at home. Washington should also be able to have a successful offensive day, which makes the over the play. Padres vs Nationals prediction: Over 7.5 (-110) available at time of publishing. Playable to -115. You can bet on our Padres vs Nationals pick with BetMGM , where right now new users can activate your new BetMGM account ⚡ and receive $150 in bonus bets INSTANTLY if your first $10+ wager is a winner!

I find Saturday’s matchup between the Toronto Blue Jays and the Baltimore Orioles to be a compelling spot. I do my handicapping before looking at betting lines, and then I try to locate the best value from there. I initially circled the under for this matchup but was surprised to see the total already at 7.5. That is a low mark, in my opinion, so I will instead pivot to a side. Trey Yesavage is coming off his worst outing of the season. He has only made 6 starts so far, but he allowed 5 earned runs across 6.2 innings against the Marlins last time out. His first 5 starts had been very strong, allowing just 3 combined earned runs in that sample. Baltimore is a solid offense against right-handed pitching, especially in their home park, but I am a fan of Yesavage and his profile. He ranks as one of the premier pitchers when it comes to suppressing hard contact, and his strikeout numbers are terrific as well. Brandon Young is also pitching well entering this game on Saturday, as he has a 2.91 ERA across his first 21.2 innings this month. Baltimore is 4-0 in those games thanks to some solid run support, but Young is showing some concerning signs. His 11 walks to 17 strikeouts in that span is not a great ratio, and on the season his K-BB% sits at just 6.1%. Young struggles to keep the ball on the ground and that is concerning with the hard contact he allows. I side with Toronto at current pricing. Blue Jays vs Orioles prediction: Toronto Blue Jays ML (-122) available at time of publishing. Playable to -125. You can bet on our Blue Jays vs Orioles pick with BetMGM , where right now new users can activate your new BetMGM account ⚡ and receive $150 in bonus bets INSTANTLY if your first $10+ wager is a winner!

The Red Sox fell behind early on Friday night, surrendering 4 first-inning runs to the Guardians, and never fully recovering in a 4-3 loss. Boston will look to even the series on Saturday when they hand the ball to one of their most reliable arms, Sonny Gray. Gray enters with a 5-1 record, 3.27 ERA and 1.20 WHIP this season. Cleveland will hand the ball to left-hander Parker Messick, who enters with a 6-1 record, 2.24 ERA and a 1.04 WHIP. On paper, this is a fantastic pitching matchup. Gray has been excellent for Boston, but Messick has been one of the most consistent starters in baseball this season. The biggest concern for Messick is that Boston has hit left-handed pitching well all year, batting .274 against southpaws. The Red Sox have also been swinging hot bats lately, averaging 4.6 runs per game while hitting .290 over their last 10. The Guardians offense is averaging only 2.6 runs and hitting .224 in that same span. However, Cleveland’s success this season has been built around strong pitching, and Messick has been a major reason why. While Boston’s offense presents a challenge, the Guardians have done a great job at limiting scoring opportunities, and we saw that last night. With Cleveland holding the edge on the mound and Boston losing six of their last 10 games, I like Cleveland to get the job done at home. Red Sox vs Guardians prediction: Cleveland Guardians ML (-130) at the time of publishing. Playable to -140. The Tampa Bay Rays, despite losing 4 consecutive games entering this series against the Los Angeles Angels, still sit at the top of the American League East with a sterling 35-19 record. Things haven’t been going as well for the Angels this season, and that continued in the first game of the series on Friday night when Tampa Bay escaped with an 8-5 victory. Angels pitchers have been struggling to keep runs off the board this season, and that could continue on Saturday with Reid Detmers taking the mound. The lefty had an unbelievable outing last time out, striking out 14 and allowing just 1 hit and 1 earned run over 8 innings, but his overall form this season has been rough — including when he allowed 8 earned runs in the outing prior to that dazzling 8-inning performance. Drew Rasmussen has been pitching quite well for the Rays’ stingy staff this season, with his most recent outing being a 7-inning gem against the New York Yankees, allowing just 5 hits and no earned runs over 7 innings. Detmers will be riding high after his last start, but the Rays are one of the best hitting teams against lefties this season, and the Angels’ starter gives no reason to believe he can string together 2 solid outings. Angels vs Rays prediction: Tampa Bay Rays ML (-165) available at time of publishing. Playable to -180. The New York Mets exacted a bit of revenge on the Miami Marlins on Friday night, taking the first game of the series 9-7. The Marlins swept the Mets at home in a 3-game series last week, with New York looking to do the same this weekend. The Mets will send Christian Scott to the mound to attempt to get one step closer to that goal, while the Marlins will counter with Tyler Phillips as he continues to transition into the rotation. Phillips has been performing well for Miami in his last 2 outings, slowly building himself up to more of a starter’s workload. He went at least 3 innings in each of those appearances, allowing just 4 hits and no earned runs over 6.2 combined innings. The Marlins’ bullpen might still need to take the bulk of this game, which is not too concerning considering they are a top-10 group in baseball. Scott is another pitcher that doesn’t go too deep into games but has been performing well for the Mets this season. Both of these pitchers will give their respective teams a solid first half of the game, while New York is another team with a top-10 bullpen. After a run-scoring onslaught in Friday night’s game, expect a bit more of a pitching duel in this matchup. Marlins vs Mets prediction: Under 7 (-105) available at time of publishing. Brandon Sproat (5.84 ERA, 1.54 WHIP) takes the mound for the Brewers on the road, while Peter Lambert (3.79 ERA, 1.17 WHIP) takes the bump for the hosting Astros. Both pitchers have something in common: they’ve each given up at least 3 earned runs in 3 consecutive starts. While Milwaukee has a solid bullpen in relief late in the game, Sproat has some daunting underlying numbers — falling in the 2nd percentile in pitching run value with a poor 4.81 xERA that ranks 24th percentile. Lambert has slightly better figures, though the Houston bullpen is a major problem, sitting dead last in MLB with a 5.32 combined ERA. Both offenses are above league average in wRC+ and OBP, with the Brewers sitting top-10 in both categories. The pitching matchup in this one calls for plenty of scoring opportunities for both sides, so I have this one going over 8.5 runs. Brewers vs Astros prediction: Over 8.5 (-115) at time of publishing. Brady Singer has had a rough go in 2026, pitching to a 6.26 ERA in his first 46 innings of work. Singer is averaging just 4.6 innings per start thanks to opponents having a .980 OPS against him. In fact, Left-handed batters have a 1.105 OPS against him, and Atlanta is projected to have 6 lefty bats in their lineup on Saturday. Singer has one of the more concerning baseball savant pages, as he is filling the zone but allowing a 44.3% hard-hit rate and a 13.0% barrel rate. He has allowed 11 home runs in his last 5 starts alone, and so far in 17 innings this month, he has posted an 8.47 ERA across 4 starts. Atlanta ranks 2nd in baseball in wOBA against right-handed pitching, while posting a wRC+ of 118. Cincinnati has been a productive offense against left-handed pitching this season. Entering Saturday, the Reds have the 6th-best wOBA against lefties, while posting a 109 wRC+. They will face off against veteran southpaw Martin Perez and his 2.70 ERA, which has been welcomed production by Atlanta through 46.2 innings. That said, he could be due for some regression. Both his xERA and FIP are well over a full run higher, and his current 83.7% strand rate is an unsustainable mark. All things considered, I favor the over on the posted game total in this game. Reds vs Braves prediction: Over 9.5 (+100) available at time of publishing. Playable to -110. Friday’s game between the Cubs and Cardinals got off to quite the start, as each team scored 3 runs in the opening inning. However, it was the Cardinals that outlasted the Cubs over the final 8 innings in a 6-5 win that included 20 combined hits and 4 home runs. The Cubs will look to even the series on Saturday when they send Ben Brown to the mound. Brown hasn’t been a starter all year, but he’s thrown at least 4 innings in each of his last 4 appearances, including 11 innings over his last 2 starts against the Pirates and Brewers. In those 4 appearances, he struck out 23 combined batters and surrendered just 4 earned runs across 19 total innings. Brown even held the hot-hitting Braves scoreless in 4 innings, striking out 7. St. Louis is projected to counter with Kyle Leahy, who had a rough outing last Saturday in Cincinnati. Leahy allowed 5 runs on 7 hits, 2 home runs and 2 walks, making it the 3rd time in his 10 starts that he has allowed at least 4 runs. Despite the poor showing, Leahy still has managed a 2.95 ERA in May thanks in part to just 2 earned runs allowed in 11.1 innings at home this month. I don’t see much of a starting pitching edge either way here, and the offenses both perform similarly against right-handed pitchers, so this game is a toss-up – and it’s priced as such. In fact, it might be a bit heavy in Chicago’s favor. Give me St. Louis at home on the plus-money price. Cubs vs Cardinals prediction: St. Louis Cardinals ML (+115) at the time of publishing. Saturday’s pitching matchup between the Giants and Rockies pits San Francisco’s Adrian Houser against Colorado’s Ryan Feltner. Both pitchers have seen their fair share of struggles this season. On one side, Houser owns a 5.30 ERA to go along with a 1.48 WHIP through 10 starts and 52.2 innings. Houser’s home ballpark of Oracle Park is among the most pitcher-friendly stadiums out there, yet he owns a 6.97 ERA and .337 BAA in 4 home starts. On Saturday, he has to pitch at the most hitter-friendly ballpark in baseball at Coors Field. Thus, it’s fair to say Houser enters this contest with plenty of uncertainty. On the other side, Colorado’s Ryan Feltner will be making his first start since April 23 after spending more than a month on the IL with an elbow injury. In 5 starts this season, Feltner owns a 6.30 ERA and opponents boast a solid .903 OPS against him. Neither Houser nor Feltner have been racking up the strikeouts, which means the ball should be put in play early and often. That type of formula tends to result in high-scoring games at Coors Field. Look to the over in this NL West matchup. Giants vs Rockies prediction: Over 11 (-105) available at time of publishing. Playable to -115. The New York Yankees continued their hot streak Friday night, defeating the Athletics 8-2 behind an 11-hit performance. Winners of 5 straight, the Yankees will look to keep the momentum rolling when they hand the ball to the left-hander Ryan Weathers, who enters with a 2-2 record, 3.14 ERA and 1.12 WHIP. The last time Weathers faced the A’s, he picked up the loss despite allowing just 1 earned run on 7 hits in 8 innings pitched. For the Athletics, J.T. Ginn will get the nod, entering with a 2-3 record, 3.19 ERA and 1.14 WHIP. The Athletics are batting .239 against lefties this season, and they failed to generate much offense against Weathers in their previous matchup. Meanwhile, the Yankees offense is surging right now, averaging 5.1 runs per game and hitting .279, compared to the A’s at 3.7 runs and .230. It’s too hard to trust the A’s in this spot, and while Ginn has pitched well this season, the Yankees are seeing the ball extremely well right now. Look for the Yankees to keep rolling and pick up another victory on the road. Yankees vs Athletics prediction: New York Yankees ML (-155) at the time of publishing. Playable up to – 175. The Arizona Diamondbacks and Seattle Mariners have one of the final games on the slate tonight. They battled late into the night on Friday before the Mariners eventually took a 7-6 victory in extra innings. That pushed the Mariners’ winning streak to 4 games, and it ended a 5-game streak for the D-Backs. As we head to Saturday, the Mariners are heavy favorites with Bryan Woo on the mound as he faces Ryne Nelson. There is value in backing the Diamondbacks at a nice plus-money payout, but I am going to go with under 7 in this spot. We don’t usually want to bet an under with Ryne Nelson on the mound, as he is very capable of giving up a big number. However, he has been really strong in the month of May, pitching into the 7th or 8th inning in 4 straight starts, logging quality starts in all of them. He will face a Seattle lineup that has never seen him before, and feels a little thin without Cal Raliegh in the middle of the order. Woo, on the other side, is as reliable as a starter gets. He has had a couple of rough outings this season, but he has more than earned my trust whenever he takes the hill. I think we could be in for a low-scoring grind of a game. The books think so too, and they know we hate betting unders already. Diamondbacks vs Mariners Prediction : Under 7 (-115) available at the time of publishing. Playable at that number . The Philadelphia Phillies and Los Angeles Dodgers square off in what looks like one of the premier matchups on the Saturday slate, but despite the star power on both sides, the value may lie with a lower-scoring game. Philadelphia sends Jesus Luzardo to the mound, and while his 4-4 record and 4.38 ERA do not immediately jump off the page, the left hander still possesses some of the best swing-and-miss stuff in the National League. Luzardo has recorded strikeouts at a high rate throughout the season and has shown the ability to neutralize powerful lineups when his fastball and breaking pitches are working together. The Phillies have also been involved in a large number of lower-scoring games recently, thanks in part to a bullpen that has quietly become one of the more reliable units in the league. The Dodgers counter with Roki Sasaki, who enters 3-3 with a 4.93 ERA. While the surface numbers suggest inconsistency, Sasaki’s electric arsenal continues to give him one of the highest ceilings in baseball. His strikeout ability remains elite, and he has often pitched better than the results indicate when avoiding free passes. Los Angeles remains one of the most dangerous offenses in the league, but the Dodgers have also trended toward lower-scoring games recently, as their pitching staff and bullpen have settled into form. The betting market has shown heavy support for both Los Angeles and the over, but the total remaining at 8.5 despite that action is notable. With both teams carrying strong under trends, 2 pitchers capable of generating swing-and-misses and playoff caliber bullpens available behind them, this matchup sets up as a tightly-contested game in which runs could be much harder to find than the public expects. Phillies vs Dodgers Prediction: Under 8.5 (-115) available at time of publishing. Playable to 8. The early game on Saturday features the Chicago White Sox vs the Detroit Tigers. On Friday, the White Sox walked off Detroit in the 10th inning, and that was after a 9th-inning rally to force extras. At some point, we need to start taking the White Sox seriously. The Tigers, on the other hand, are cratering hard. They have lost 11 of their last 13 games, and blowing the lead twice in the final 2 innings on Friday is just demoralizing. I have no idea why the White Sox are such plus-money underdogs, but I am happy to see it, and I will be locking that in. The pitching matchup features a pair of lefties, Framber Valdez and Anthony Kay. Both have been pitching well for most of the season, and there is no reason to fade either guy. What does stand out, however, is the slugging percentage of each team vs lefties. Would it surprise you to know that the White Sox have the 4th-best slugging percentage in the league vs LHP? They trail only the Yankees, Dodgers, and Diamondbacks. The Tigers are in the bottom 5 in the same category. Look, the White Sox keep finding ways to win, and the Tigers keep finding ways to lose. Let’s ride the hot team. Tigers vs White Sox Prediction : Chicago White Sox ML (+115) available at the time of publishing. Playable to -110 . Detroit is 2-8 over its last 10, with 5 of those 8 losses coming by at least 2 runs.

The Texas Rangers will look to keep their offense rolling after a dominant 9-1 victory over the Royals on Friday night. Kansas City’s offense has been difficult to trust lately, averaging just 2.3 runs per game while batting .203 over its last 10 games. It hasn’t just been the offense that’s struggled either, as the Royals have posted a 5.46 team ERA during that stretch. The Rangers have been much more productive lately, averaging 4.7 runs per game while hitting .240 and carrying a slightly better 4.31 ERA. Taking the mound for the Royals will be Seth Lugo, who enters with a 2-4 record, 3.74 ERA and 1.38 WHIP. For the Rangers, Kumar Rocker will get the nod, entering with a 2-5 record, 3.96 ERA and 1.36 WHIP. It’s tough to back either starter in this spot, but if Rocker runs into trouble early, Texas has a significant advantage in the bullpen. The Rangers relievers own a solid 3.11 ERA compared to Kansas City’s 5.08 bullpen ERA. While I’m not entirely confident in Rocker, I do trust this Rangers bullpen and offense a lot more than the Royals right now. Look for the Rangers to get the job done at home as the Royals struggles continue. Royals vs Rangers prediction: Texas Rangers (-125) at the time of publishing. Playable to -135. The Miami Marlins are tied for 27th in home runs and have posted an ISO of .117 against right-handed pitching this month, ranking them 29th in the majors.

The New York Mets exacted a bit of revenge on the Miami Marlins on Friday night, taking the first game of the series 9-7. The Marlins swept the Mets at home in a 3-game series last week, with New York looking to do the same this weekend. The Mets will send Christian Scott to the mound to attempt to get one step closer to that goal, while the Marlins will counter with Tyler Phillips as he continues to transition into the rotation. Phillips has been performing well for Miami in his last 2 outings, slowly building himself up to more of a starter’s workload. He went at least 3 innings in each of those appearances, allowing just 4 hits and no earned runs over 6.2 combined innings. The Marlins’ bullpen might still need to take the bulk of this game, which is not too concerning considering they are a top-10 group in baseball. Scott is another pitcher that doesn’t go too deep into games but has been performing well for the Mets this season. Both of these pitchers will give their respective teams a solid first half of the game, while New York is another team with a top-10 bullpen. After a run-scoring onslaught in Friday night’s game, expect a bit more of a pitching duel in this matchup. Marlins vs Mets prediction: Under 7 (-105) available at time of publishing. Jesus Luzardo has superb underlying metrics and strong prior results against LA's lineup (.168 AVG, .558 OPS). Both pens have been dominant (Philadelphia 2.07 SIERA, LA 2.96 FIP in the last 20 days) and are very well rested.

The Philadelphia Phillies and Los Angeles Dodgers square off in what looks like one of the premier matchups on the Saturday slate, but despite the star power on both sides, the value may lie with a lower-scoring game. Philadelphia sends Jesus Luzardo to the mound, and while his 4-4 record and 4.38 ERA do not immediately jump off the page, the left hander still possesses some of the best swing-and-miss stuff in the National League. Luzardo has recorded strikeouts at a high rate throughout the season and has shown the ability to neutralize powerful lineups when his fastball and breaking pitches are working together. The Phillies have also been involved in a large number of lower-scoring games recently, thanks in part to a bullpen that has quietly become one of the more reliable units in the league. The Dodgers counter with Roki Sasaki, who enters 3-3 with a 4.93 ERA. While the surface numbers suggest inconsistency, Sasaki’s electric arsenal continues to give him one of the highest ceilings in baseball. His strikeout ability remains elite, and he has often pitched better than the results indicate when avoiding free passes. Los Angeles remains one of the most dangerous offenses in the league, but the Dodgers have also trended toward lower-scoring games recently, as their pitching staff and bullpen have settled into form. The betting market has shown heavy support for both Los Angeles and the over, but the total remaining at 8.5 despite that action is notable. With both teams carrying strong under trends, 2 pitchers capable of generating swing-and-misses and playoff caliber bullpens available behind them, this matchup sets up as a tightly-contested game in which runs could be much harder to find than the public expects. Phillies vs Dodgers Prediction: Under 8.5 (-115) available at time of publishing. Playable to 8. examples.analysis: A converted reliever, Brown started his last four, never allowing more than three runs. All Brown's stuff ranks in the top third of MLB, including a 99th-percentile breaking ball. He'll face St. Louis, which scored two runs in three games before Friday. The Cardinals start Kyle Leahy, who ranks in the bottom third in every pitch and is worse than 92% of MLB pitchers in overall run value.

The Red Sox fell behind early on Friday night, surrendering 4 first-inning runs to the Guardians, and never fully recovering in a 4-3 loss. Boston will look to even the series on Saturday when they hand the ball to one of their most reliable arms, Sonny Gray. Gray enters with a 5-1 record, 3.27 ERA and 1.20 WHIP this season. Cleveland will hand the ball to left-hander Parker Messick, who enters with a 6-1 record, 2.24 ERA and a 1.04 WHIP. On paper, this is a fantastic pitching matchup. Gray has been excellent for Boston, but Messick has been one of the most consistent starters in baseball this season. The biggest concern for Messick is that Boston has hit left-handed pitching well all year, batting .274 against southpaws. The Red Sox have also been swinging hot bats lately, averaging 4.6 runs per game while hitting .290 over their last 10. The Guardians offense is averaging only 2.6 runs and hitting .224 in that same span. However, Cleveland’s success this season has been built around strong pitching, and Messick has been a major reason why. While Boston’s offense presents a challenge, the Guardians have done a great job at limiting scoring opportunities, and we saw that last night. With Cleveland holding the edge on the mound and Boston losing six of their last 10 games, I like Cleveland to get the job done at home. The New York Mets have the pitching advantage with Christian Scott taking the bump. He owns a 2.8 FIP and 3.0 ERA over the past month despite a .328 batting average on balls put in play, which should drop moving forward.

The New York Yankees continued their hot streak Friday night, defeating the Athletics 8-2 behind an 11-hit performance. Winners of 5 straight, the Yankees will look to keep the momentum rolling when they hand the ball to the left-hander Ryan Weathers, who enters with a 2-2 record, 3.14 ERA and 1.12 WHIP. The last time Weathers faced the A’s, he picked up the loss despite allowing just 1 earned run on 7 hits in 8 innings pitched. For the Athletics, J.T. Ginn will get the nod, entering with a 2-3 record, 3.19 ERA and 1.14 WHIP. The Athletics are batting .239 against lefties this season, and they failed to generate much offense against Weathers in their previous matchup. Meanwhile, the Yankees offense is surging right now, averaging 5.1 runs per game and hitting .279, compared to the A’s at 3.7 runs and .230. It’s too hard to trust the A’s in this spot, and while Ginn has pitched well this season, the Yankees are seeing the ball extremely well right now. Look for the Yankees to keep rolling and pick up another victory on the road. Yankees vs Athletics prediction: New York Yankees ML (-155) at the time of publishing. Playable up to – 175. Saturday’s pitching matchup between the Giants and Rockies pits San Francisco’s Adrian Houser against Colorado’s Ryan Feltner. Both pitchers have seen their fair share of struggles this season. On one side, Houser owns a 5.30 ERA to go along with a 1.48 WHIP through 10 starts and 52.2 innings. Houser’s home ballpark of Oracle Park is among the most pitcher-friendly stadiums out there, yet he owns a 6.97 ERA and .337 BAA in 4 home starts. On Saturday, he has to pitch at the most hitter-friendly ballpark in baseball at Coors Field. Thus, it’s fair to say Houser enters this contest with plenty of uncertainty. On the other side, Colorado’s Ryan Feltner will be making his first start since April 23 after spending more than a month on the IL with an elbow injury. In 5 starts this season, Feltner owns a 6.30 ERA and opponents boast a solid .903 OPS against him. Neither Houser nor Feltner have been racking up the strikeouts, which means the ball should be put in play early and often. That type of formula tends to result in high-scoring games at Coors Field. Look to the over in this NL West matchup. Giants vs Rockies prediction: Over 11 (-105) available at time of publishing. Playable to -115. Kansas City has the worst bullpen WHIP in baseball and the third-worst ERA. The pen allowed four homers in the last three games, posting an 8.71 ERA. The Rangers have been better in relief, but Texas allowed 5+ five times in the last eight games.

The Padres and Nationals are set for a Saturday afternoon matchup at Nationals Park in the nation’s capital. After 12 total runs were scored in the opening game of this series on Friday night in San Diego’s 7-5 win, Saturday’s contest could also be of the high-scoring variety. Washington will send right-hander Foster Griffin to the mound, and he is still adjusting to life as a starting pitcher in the big leagues. This is Griffin’s first year as a full-time starter, and he has experienced some ups-and-downs through 2 months of the season. He posted a strong 2.51 ERA in April, but that number has jumped to 4.76 across his 5 starts in May. Given his inconsistencies, look for the Padres to have a strong date at the plate in this one. Michael King will toe the rubber for the visitors. King has been a steady force on the mound this season, as he enters this matchup with a 2.76 ERA and 1.15 WHIP through 11 starts and 62.0 innings of work. However, he has a tough assignment on his hands. The Nationals have been among the best offenses in baseball so far this season, as they enter this matchup ranked 1st in total runs (311) and 4th in OPS (.745). In addition, the Nationals are scoring 5.5 runs per game at home. Washington should also be able to have a successful offensive day, which makes the over the play. Padres vs Nationals prediction: Over 7.5 (-110) available at time of publishing. Playable to -115. Brandon Sproat (5.84 ERA, 1.54 WHIP) takes the mound for the Brewers on the road, while Peter Lambert (3.79 ERA, 1.17 WHIP) takes the bump for the hosting Astros. Both pitchers have something in common: they’ve each given up at least 3 earned runs in 3 consecutive starts. While Milwaukee has a solid bullpen in relief late in the game, Sproat has some daunting underlying numbers — falling in the 2nd percentile in pitching run value with a poor 4.81 xERA that ranks 24th percentile. Lambert has slightly better figures, though the Houston bullpen is a major problem, sitting dead last in MLB with a 5.32 combined ERA. Both offenses are above league average in wRC+ and OBP, with the Brewers sitting top-10 in both categories. The pitching matchup in this one calls for plenty of scoring opportunities for both sides, so I have this one going over 8.5 runs. Brewers vs Astros prediction: Over 8.5 (-115) at time of publishing. Brady Singer has had a rough go in 2026, pitching to a 6.26 ERA in his first 46 innings of work. Singer is averaging just 4.6 innings per start thanks to opponents having a .980 OPS against him. In fact, Left-handed batters have a 1.105 OPS against him, and Atlanta is projected to have 6 lefty bats in their lineup on Saturday. Singer has one of the more concerning baseball savant pages, as he is filling the zone but allowing a 44.3% hard-hit rate and a 13.0% barrel rate. He has allowed 11 home runs in his last 5 starts alone, and so far in 17 innings this month, he has posted an 8.47 ERA across 4 starts. Atlanta ranks 2nd in baseball in wOBA against right-handed pitching, while posting a wRC+ of 118. Cincinnati has been a productive offense against left-handed pitching this season. Entering Saturday, the Reds have the 6th-best wOBA against lefties, while posting a 109 wRC+. They will face off against veteran southpaw Martin Perez and his 2.70 ERA, which has been welcomed production by Atlanta through 46.2 innings. That said, he could be due for some regression. Both his xERA and FIP are well over a full run higher, and his current 83.7% strand rate is an unsustainable mark. All things considered, I favor the over on the posted game total in this game. Roki Sasaki allows loud contact (11th percentile barrel rate, 11th percentile hard-hit rate), which is a concern against a Philadelphia lineup with the fifth-highest hard-hit rate (35.6%) in the last 20 days. His four-seamer has been crushed (.348 xBA, .614 xSLG), and Philadelphia (fourth-most WAR against fastballs) will be happy to oblige.

Friday’s game between the Cubs and Cardinals got off to quite the start, as each team scored 3 runs in the opening inning. However, it was the Cardinals that outlasted the Cubs over the final 8 innings in a 6-5 win that included 20 combined hits and 4 home runs. The Cubs will look to even the series on Saturday when they send Ben Brown to the mound. Brown hasn’t been a starter all year, but he’s thrown at least 4 innings in each of his last 4 appearances, including 11 innings over his last 2 starts against the Pirates and Brewers. In those 4 appearances, he struck out 23 combined batters and surrendered just 4 earned runs across 19 total innings. Brown even held the hot-hitting Braves scoreless in 4 innings, striking out 7. St. Louis is projected to counter with Kyle Leahy, who had a rough outing last Saturday in Cincinnati. Leahy allowed 5 runs on 7 hits, 2 home runs and 2 walks, making it the 3rd time in his 10 starts that he has allowed at least 4 runs. Despite the poor showing, Leahy still has managed a 2.95 ERA in May thanks in part to just 2 earned runs allowed in 11.1 innings at home this month. I don’t see much of a starting pitching edge either way here, and the offenses both perform similarly against right-handed pitchers, so this game is a toss-up – and it’s priced as such. In fact, it might be a bit heavy in Chicago’s favor. Give me St. Louis at home on the plus-money price. Cubs vs Cardinals prediction: St. Louis Cardinals ML (+115) at the time of publishing. Drew Rasmussen vs. the New York Yankees is a mismatch on paper - the righty is 4-1 with a 1.03 ERA and 51 punchouts in eight career games against New York. Coming off a thrilling comeback win on Friday, the division-leading Tampa Bay Rays are primed to keep the foot on the gas behind their dynamic arm to create more separation in the AL East.

The Tampa Bay Rays, despite losing 4 consecutive games entering this series against the Los Angeles Angels, still sit at the top of the American League East with a sterling 35-19 record. Things haven’t been going as well for the Angels this season, and that continued in the first game of the series on Friday night when Tampa Bay escaped with an 8-5 victory. Angels pitchers have been struggling to keep runs off the board this season, and that could continue on Saturday with Reid Detmers taking the mound. The lefty had an unbelievable outing last time out, striking out 14 and allowing just 1 hit and 1 earned run over 8 innings, but his overall form this season has been rough — including when he allowed 8 earned runs in the outing prior to that dazzling 8-inning performance. Drew Rasmussen has been pitching quite well for the Rays’ stingy staff this season, with his most recent outing being a 7-inning gem against the New York Yankees, allowing just 5 hits and no earned runs over 7 innings. Detmers will be riding high after his last start, but the Rays are one of the best hitting teams against lefties this season, and the Angels’ starter gives no reason to believe he can string together 2 solid outings. The Royals have been losing big all season. K.C.'s runline and moneyline records are the same, meaning that giving up the 1.5 runs against them isn't a big risk. While starter Seth Lugo started the year strong, May was rough, posting a 5.27 ERA and 1.683 WHIP in five starts.

I find Saturday’s matchup between the Toronto Blue Jays and the Baltimore Orioles to be a compelling spot. I do my handicapping before looking at betting lines, and then I try to locate the best value from there. I initially circled the under for this matchup but was surprised to see the total already at 7.5. That is a low mark, in my opinion, so I will instead pivot to a side. Trey Yesavage is coming off his worst outing of the season. He has only made 6 starts so far, but he allowed 5 earned runs across 6.2 innings against the Marlins last time out. His first 5 starts had been very strong, allowing just 3 combined earned runs in that sample. Baltimore is a solid offense against right-handed pitching, especially in their home park, but I am a fan of Yesavage and his profile. He ranks as one of the premier pitchers when it comes to suppressing hard contact, and his strikeout numbers are terrific as well. Brandon Young is also pitching well entering this game on Saturday, as he has a 2.91 ERA across his first 21.2 innings this month. Baltimore is 4-0 in those games thanks to some solid run support, but Young is showing some concerning signs. His 11 walks to 17 strikeouts in that span is not a great ratio, and on the season his K-BB% sits at just 6.1%. Young struggles to keep the ball on the ground and that is concerning with the hard contact he allows. I side with Toronto at current pricing. The Arizona Diamondbacks and Seattle Mariners have one of the final games on the slate tonight. They battled late into the night on Friday before the Mariners eventually took a 7-6 victory in extra innings. That pushed the Mariners’ winning streak to 4 games, and it ended a 5-game streak for the D-Backs. As we head to Saturday, the Mariners are heavy favorites with Bryan Woo on the mound as he faces Ryne Nelson. There is value in backing the Diamondbacks at a nice plus-money payout, but I am going to go with under 7 in this spot. We don’t usually want to bet an under with Ryne Nelson on the mound, as he is very capable of giving up a big number. However, he has been really strong in the month of May, pitching into the 7th or 8th inning in 4 straight starts, logging quality starts in all of them. He will face a Seattle lineup that has never seen him before, and feels a little thin without Cal Raliegh in the middle of the order. Woo, on the other side, is as reliable as a starter gets. He has had a couple of rough outings this season, but he has more than earned my trust whenever he takes the hill. I think we could be in for a low-scoring grind of a game. The books think so too, and they know we hate betting unders already. Diamondbacks vs Mariners Prediction : Under 7 (-115) available at the time of publishing. Playable at that number . The Cardinals' six-run outburst on Friday looks like an anomaly. Nelson Velasquez, called up earlier in the day, hit St. Louis' first home run this week. He's a platoon slugger, however, who'll likely be on the bench against a right-hander. That leaves the Cardinals' batters who have produced 0 or 1 runs in five of the last 10 games.

Neither lineup has done much damage in the first inning lately, making this NRFI appealing. Brandon Sproat hasn't allowed a first-inning run in five straight starts, while Peter Lambert owns a 6-1 NRFI record and a sub-4.00 ERA. Milwaukee is hitting just .205 in opening frames this season.

Mitch Keller and Bailey Ober have been among the most reliable first-inning pitchers in baseball this season. Keller owns a 9-2 NRFI record and hasn't allowed a first-inning run in three straight starts, while Ober is 10-1 and has gone nine consecutive outings without trouble. Both offenses also struggle early.
